Operation 'firebasedatabase.projects.locations.instances.delete' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details. Missing summary entirely. Description is comprehensive but could better clarify the grace period recovery window and explicitly state the DISABLED prerequisite as a constraint. Added summary and enhanced description to improve AI agent understanding of the deletion workflow and recovery options.

data.current_description: Marks a DatabaseInstance to be deleted. The DatabaseInstance will be set to the DELETED state for 20 days, and will be purged within 30 days. The default database cannot be deleted. IDs for deleted database instances may never be recovered or re-used. The Database may only be deleted if it is already in a DISABLED state.
data.description_suggestion: Marks a DatabaseInstance for deletion. The instance transitions to DELETED state and will be purged within 30 days. During the 20-day grace period, the instance can be recovered using UndeleteDatabase Instance. After purging, the instance ID cannot be reused. Constraints: The default database cannot be deleted, and the instance must be in DISABLED state before deletion. Note: Deleted instance IDs are permanently unavailable.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description is comprehensive but could better clarify the grace period recovery window and explicitly state the DISABLED prerequisite as a constraint. Added summary and enhanced description to improve AI agent understanding of the deletion workflow and recovery options.

message: Operation 'firebasedatabase.projects.locations.instances.disable' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_side_effects. Missing summary. Description lacks clarity on state transitions and prerequisites. Enhanced description to explicitly state the DISABLED state transition, connection termination side effects, and ACTIVE state prerequisite for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Disables a DatabaseInstance. The database can be re-enabled later using ReenableDatabaseInstance. When a database is disabled, all reads and writes are denied, including view access in the Firebase console.
data.summary_suggestion: Disable a DatabaseInstance and block all access
data.description_suggestion: Disables a DatabaseInstance, blocking all read and write operations and Firebase console access. A disabled database can be re-enabled using ReenableDatabaseInstance to restore full functionality. Side effects: All client connections are terminated, and the instance transitions to DISABLED state. This operation requires the instance to be in ACTIVE state.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description lacks clarity on state transitions and prerequisites. Enhanced description to explicitly state the DISABLED state transition, connection termination side effects, and ACTIVE state prerequisite for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'firebasedatabase.projects.locations.instances.reenable' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_side_effects. Missing summary. Description lacks explicit side effects and clarity on operational impact. Added summary and enhanced description to clarify state transitions, connection restoration, and DISABLED state prerequisite for improved AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Enables a DatabaseInstance. The database must have been disabled previously using DisableDatabaseInstance. The state of a successfully reenabled DatabaseInstance is ACTIVE.
data.description_suggestion: Re-enables a DatabaseInstance that was previously disabled using DisableDatabaseInstance. The instance transitions to ACTIVE state and resumes accepting all read and write operations. Side effects: Client connections can be re-established, and Firebase console access is restored. This operation requires the instance to be in DISABLED state.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description lacks explicit side effects and clarity on operational impact. Added summary and enhanced description to clarify state transitions, connection restoration, and DISABLED state prerequisite for improved AI agent understanding.

Description is detailed but could better emphasize the time-sensitive nature and recovery guarantee. data.current_description: Restores a DatabaseInstance that was previously marked to be deleted. After the delete method is used, DatabaseInstances are set to the DELETED state for 20 days, and will be purged within 30 days. Databases in the DELETED state can be undeleted without losing any data. This method may only be used on a DatabaseInstance in the DELETED state. Purged DatabaseInstances may not be recovered.
data.summary_suggestion: Restore a deleted DatabaseInstance within grace period
data.description_suggestion: Restores a DatabaseInstance that was marked for deletion using the delete method. The instance must be in DELETED state and within the 20-day grace period before purging. Undeleting recovers all data without loss. Side effects: The instance transitions to ACTIVE state and resumes normal operations. Constraint: Purged instances (after 30 days) cannot be recovered. This operation is only valid for instances in DELETED state.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description is detailed but could better emphasize the time-sensitive nature and recovery guarantee. Added summary highlighting the grace period constraint and enhanced description to clarify state transitions, data recovery guarantee, and purge deadline for improved AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'firebasedatabase.projects.locations.instances.list' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context. Missing summary. data.current_description: Lists each DatabaseInstance associated with the specified parent project. The list items are returned in no particular order, but will be a consistent view of the database instances when additional requests are made with a `pageToken`. The resulting list contains instances in any STATE. The list results may be stale by a few seconds. Use GetDatabaseInstance for consistent reads.
data.summary_suggestion: List all DatabaseInstances in a project
data.description_suggestion: Lists all DatabaseInstances associated with the specified parent project, including instances in any state (ACTIVE, DISABLED, DELETED). Results are returned in no particular order but provide a consistent view across paginated requests using pageToken. Note: Results may be stale by a few seconds; use GetDatabaseInstance for strongly consistent reads. Supports pagination via pageToken and pageSize parameters.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description lacks clarity on pagination support and consistency guarantees. Added action-oriented summary and enhanced description to explicitly mention pagination parameters, state filtering behavior, and consistency model for better AI agent understanding of when to use this operation versus GetDatabaseInstance.

data.current_description: Requests that a new DatabaseInstance be created. The state of a successfully created DatabaseInstance is ACTIVE. Only available for projects on the Blaze plan. Projects can be upgraded using the Cloud Billing API https://cloud.google.com/billing/reference/rest/v1/projects/updateBillingInfo. Note that it might take a few minutes for billing enablement state to propagate to Firebase systems.
data.summary_suggestion: Create a new DatabaseInstance in a project
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new DatabaseInstance in the specified project. The instance transitions to ACTIVE state upon successful creation. Input: Provide a DatabaseInstance resource with desired configuration (name, region, etc.). Constraints: Only available for projects on the Blaze billing plan. Projects can be upgraded via Cloud Billing API. Side effects: A new database instance is provisioned and becomes immediately available. Note: Billing enablement state may take a few minutes to propagate to Firebase systems.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description lacks clarity on input requirements and explicit side effects. Added action-oriented summary and enhanced description to specify input schema expectations, billing plan constraints, and provisioning side effects for improved AI agent understanding of prerequisites and outcomes.
